hero.headline: Where a repo-first harness earns its place
hero.body: Eight situations where governance, persistence and coordination matter more than raw generation.

useCases.name: New Repository
useCases.title: New Repository
useCases.description: Governance structure from the first commit: domains, agents, autonomy and gates exist before the first feature does.

useCases.name: Legacy Repository
useCases.title: Legacy Repository
useCases.description: Progressive discovery without rebuilding the project — the harness proposes domains from the structure that already exists.

useCases.name: Monorepo
useCases.title: Monorepo
useCases.description: Different responsibilities for different areas and products: path-based choreography gives each territory its own agents, advisors and gates.

useCases.name: Regulated Engineering
useCases.title: Regulated Engineering
useCases.description: Specs, evidence, executable gates and human approval — an audit trail that exists because the workflow produces it, not as an afterthought.

useCases.name: Multi-agent Engineering
useCases.title: Multi-agent Engineering
useCases.description: Explicit coordination between operational and consultative agents: roles, boundaries and shared signals instead of overlapping sessions.

useCases.name: Consulting
useCases.title: Consulting
useCases.description: A common kernel with client-specific overlays: the same governance machinery, adapted per engagement without forking the practice.

useCases.name: Product Platform
useCases.title: Product Platform
useCases.description: Shared policies across different products: one policy surface, many repositories, consistent gates and autonomy rules.

useCases.name: Model Migration
useCases.title: Model Migration
useCases.description: Swap models and tools while context, procedures and governance stay in the repository — nothing to re-teach, nothing lost.
